title: Road Closure Fee Schedule Debate — Precedent and Budget Deficit Tensions
summary: Council scrutinizes the fee structure for Syracuse University's unprecedented 2-year Comstock Avenue road closure. Fees paid so far: $75 liability waiver and $1,500/meter for parking meter removal. The road cut permit — which triggers the compounding 14-day renewal fee schedule — has not been issued. Under current rules, fees could reach ~$2.6M for the full closure. Council raises concerns about precedent-setting: legislation should apply uniformly to all developers. A councilor invokes the city's $20M+ projected budget deficit, questioning whether fee modifications for development feasibility can be balanced against the city's own fiscal needs. Administration requests 90 more days (through end of June) to develop an approach for full road closures going forward.

political_signal.description: Council closely questions fee modification approach, raises precedent-setting concerns and invokes $20M budget deficit. Councilor: 'we're setting the precedent for what happens in the future' and 'we have a $20 million projected if not more deficit.'

processing_notes: $20 detected as dollar amount but refers to '$20 million projected deficit'
$75 and $1,500 are per the liability waiver fee schedule
